name: Ida Cox
description: African American singer and vaudeville performer (1896-1967)
abstract: Ida M. Cox (née Prather; February 26, 1888 or 1896 – November 10, 1967) was an American singer and vaudeville performer, best known for her blues performances and recordings.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave S. Oxley Sr.
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Oh, I was going to ask about Ida Cox. Did you ever tour with her? >> [INTERVIEWEE] Oh, yes, indeed. I was with Ida Cox in 1940.

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: We had made a connection to work with Ida Cox... We would travel all down through the South
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Billie Pierce and De De Pierce
about_quotes.evidence: Oh yeah, I went on a road with Ida. ... About twelve years ago I was on the road with Ida.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billie Pierce and De De Pierce Interview, 1959-10-07

about_quotes.speaker: Billie Pierce and De De Pierce
about_quotes.evidence: I didn't think too much of Ida Cox singing, I worked with Ida.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billie Pierce and De De Pierce Interview, 1959-04-02

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: I just felt that I was above that. A show came to Dallas. I went to Tulsa; from Tulsa to Oklahoma City and from Oklahoma City to Kansas City. And I stayed in Kansas City from '29 to '32. ... you seen Ida Cox and Lovey Taylor and Trixie Smith and Mamie Smith

about_quotes.speaker: George Mitchell
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer asks: 'been you ever work with her...Ida Cox...' Interviewee does not confirm working with her, instead discussing general recording habits and other singers like Maureenie.
